title: The Real Difference Between Your Choices for Selling Plugins Without a Payment Processor — The Full Guide
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal and Stripe block creators in dozens of countries. Here is the infrastructure that works everywhere they don't.

content.text: You're a digital creator trying to sell plugins online, but PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, or Payhip won't let you. They've shut you out due to banking restrictions, geographic limitations, or anti-money laundering laws in your country. You need a way to sell your plugins without getting blocked by payment processors. Your options are limited, but one solution stands out: using a crypto-native infrastructure that bypasses traditional payment processing entirely.
What Each Option Actually Offers

PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip are all popular platforms for selling digital products, but they each have their own set of limitations and requirements. PayPal, for example, requires a verified account and can freeze your funds for months due to compliance issues. Stripe is more restrictive in certain countries, like Nigeria and Ghana, due to banking restrictions. Gumroad is blocked in several countries, including Iran and Venezuela. Payhip has a more open policy but still requires a verified bank account. Meanwhile, a crypto-native infrastructure like Core Shop Engine offers a full backend with automated delivery, payment verification, and PDF receipts, without the need for a payment processor account or any geographic restrictions.
How They Handle Restricted Countries

The main problem with PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip is that they reserve the right to restrict access to certain countries due to compliance issues or banking restrictions. This means that creators from Nigeria, Ghana, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Ethiopia, Venezuela, Iran, and other countries may be blocked from selling their digital products on these platforms. In contrast, a crypto-native infrastructure like Core Shop Engine works everywhere, without any restrictions or limitations.
Payment Speed, Fees, and Real Costs

Traditional payment processors like PayPal and Stripe charge high fees for transactions, often as high as 3.5% + $0.30 per transaction. They also reserve the right to freeze your funds for months due to compliance issues. In contrast, a crypto-native infrastructure like Core Shop Engine offers instant payment verification, with no fees or frozen funds. You get your money immediately, without any delays or deductions.
Crypto-native infrastructure offers instant payment verification with no fees or frozen funds.
Automation and Delivery Capabilities

A crypto-native infrastructure like Core Shop Engine offers full automation and delivery capabilities, including automated delivery of digital products, payment verification, and PDF receipts. This means you can focus on creating and selling your plugins, without worrying about the technical details of payment processing and delivery. You can even set up automated email notifications and follow-up sequences to engage with your customers and promote your products.
Which One to Choose Based on Your Situation

If you're a digital creator trying to sell plugins online, but facing restrictions from PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, or Payhip, a crypto-native infrastructure like Core Shop Engine is the clear choice. It offers full automation and delivery capabilities, instant payment verification, and no fees or frozen funds. You can work everywhere, without any geographic restrictions or limitations. And with its low cost and easy setup, you can start selling your plugins online today, without any hassle or complications.
How do I sell digital products if PayPal is not available in my country?

The direct answer is NOWPayments combined with the Core Shop Engine. NOWPayments is a crypto payment processor that requires no KYC from merchants and no account creation from buyers. It supports over 300 coins and sends a webhook to your backend when a payment confirms on-chain. The Core Shop Engine receives that webhook, verifies the transaction, generates a receipt, and delivers the product automatically. The key difference between this and a platform workaround is that it is not a workaround — it is a different infrastructure layer entirely. There is no platform that can suspend your access, no compliance team that can flag your account, and no payout schedule that delays your revenue. Your funds go directly to a wallet you control the moment a transaction confirms.
Traditional payment processors like PayPal and Stripe charge high fees for transactions and reserve the right to freeze your funds.
